fact: First Step: The Regulating Act of 1773 was the first step taken by the British Parliament to control the East India Company.
heading: 1773 Act

fact: First Supreme Court: Established at Calcutta in 1774 under the Regulating Act of 1773, with Sir Elijah Impey as Chief Justice.
heading: Supreme Court

fact: Crown Rule: The Government of India Act 1858 transferred power directly to the British Crown, ending Company rule.
heading: Crown Rule

fact: Communal Electorate: Introduced by the Indian Councils Act 1909 (Minto-Morley Reforms) for Muslims.
heading: Communal Electorate

fact: Blueprint: The Government of India Act 1935 provided the primary structural blueprint for the Indian Constitution.
heading: 1935 Act

fact: Magna Carta: Part III of the Constitution (Fundamental Rights) is rightly described as the 'Magna Carta of India'.
heading: Magna Carta

fact: Citizens Only: Articles 15, 16, 19, 29, and 30 are available EXCLUSIVELY to Indian citizens.
heading: Citizens Only

fact: Un-suspendable: Articles 20 and 21 cannot be suspended even during a National Emergency (Article 352).
heading: Un-suspendable

fact: Property Right: The Right to Property (Article 31) was deleted from Fundamental Rights by the 44th Amendment Act (1978).
heading: Property Right

fact: Judicial Review: Article 13 provides the basis for Judicial Review, making laws inconsistent with FRs void.
heading: Judicial Review

fact: Heart & Soul: Article 32 is called the 'Heart and Soul' of the Constitution by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar.
heading: Heart & Soul

fact: RTE: Article 21A (Right to Education) was added by the 86th Constitutional Amendment Act in 2002.
heading: Right to Education
